issues.main_description_html:
Sub-tasks

  • Update Payload for blur_requests: Additionally, fix the messaging to the blur_requests queue. The expected payload must look like this: 
    {
  "pano_id": "47434272",
  "types": ["face"]
}

Note from Developer

The data structure sent is in accordance with what was requested, but blur still not working, here the video:

Screen.Recording.2026-06-17.155911.mp4

Description

Error 500 returned by the endpoint:

GET https://apil.gothru.co/v1/tours/status/extra-pano?mid=3491846
Request Method GET
Status Code 500 Internal Server Error

Response payload

{
    "statusCode": 500,
    "data": {
        "messages": "An error occurred (google photos): {\n  \"error\": {\n    \"code\": 403,\n    \"message\": \"Request had insufficient authentication scopes.\",\n    \"errors\": [\n      {\n        \"message\": \"Insufficient Permission\",\n        \"domain\": \"global\",\n        \"reason\": \"insufficientPermissions\"\n      }\n    ],\n    \"status\": \"PERMISSION_DENIED\",\n    \"details\": [\n      {\n        \"@type\": \"type.googleapis.com/google.rpc.ErrorInfo\",\n        \"reason\": \"ACCESS_TOKEN_SCOPE_INSUFFICIENT\",\n        \"domain\": \"googleapis.com\",\n        \"metadata\": {\n          \"service\": \"streetviewpublish.googleapis.com\",\n          \"method\": \"google.streetview.publish.v1.StreetViewPublishService.ListPhotos\"\n        }\n      }\n    ]\n  }\n}\n"
    }
}

Analysis

The underlying Google API returns a 403 PERMISSION_DENIED / ACCESS_TOKEN_SCOPE_INSUFFICIENT for streetviewpublish.googleapis.com → StreetViewPublishService.ListPhotos. The OAuth access token used to call the Street View Publish API is missing the required scope (https://www.googleapis.com/auth/streetviewpublish).

Two problems to address:

  1. Scope issue: The Google account/token used for this request lacks the Street View Publish scope (token needs re-consent/refresh with the correct scope, or the wrong account is linked).
  2. Error handling: A downstream 403 from Google is surfaced to the client as an unhandled 500. The endpoint should map the Google permission error to a proper status (e.g. 403/4xx) with a clear, actionable message instead of a generic 500.

Acceptance Criteria

  1. The endpoint GET /v1/tours/status/extra-pano?mid=... no longer returns a 500 when the Google Street View Publish token has insufficient scope.
  2. Insufficient-scope / permission errors from Google are caught and returned with an appropriate status code and a clear message prompting re-authentication.
  3. Re-authenticating the Google account with the correct streetviewpublish scope resolves the underlying 403 and the endpoint returns valid data.

Reference: docs/brainstom/analyze-all.md — Google Street View Publisher API dependency is flagged as the platform's highest-severity risk (§6.2). Relates to OAuth scope handling for Street View publishing.

Note from Developer

This error occurs because the street view account is not connected to the gothru account,,, but the error was too general before, but I have fixed it to be like this

issues.display_title: Issue #1300: Investigate Rogue Producer Sending Jobs to Deprecated auto_blur Queue
issues.main_description_html:
Description

An issue was identified in the RabbitMQ message broker. The queue named auto_blur, which has been intentionally shut down/deprecated, is still receiving new jobs.

This indicates that there is leftover legacy code (a producer) somewhere in the application that is still actively routing blur requests to this old queue instead of the correct, newly implemented queues.

Sub-tasks

  • Audit Codebase: Perform a global search across the backend repositories for any publishing logic or exchange bindings targeting the auto_blur routing key/queue.
  • Reroute or Remove: Update the rogue publisher to send messages to the correct active queue, or remove the legacy code entirely if that specific background job is no longer needed.
  • Queue Cleanup: Once the source code is patched and deployed, purge the remaining dead messages from the auto_blur queue and safely delete the queue from RabbitMQ to prevent further confusion.

Acceptance Criteria

  1. The auto_blur queue no longer receives any new messages during normal application usage.
  2. Legacy code referencing auto_blur is successfully removed or refactored.
  3. The RabbitMQ dashboard confirms a 0 incoming message rate for this specific queue over an extended period.

Reference: docs/brainstom/analyze-all.md — RabbitMQ workers handle blur, stitching, nadir patching, and imports (see Architecture snapshot §2.2). Related to the active pano_blur_new queue work.

Note from Developer

worker "auto_blur" was used in api /panoramas/auto-blur and this api was used in page my-tours/:mid/blur/manual in app gothru, but now the page and api have been removed
